blocked_models.blocker_reason: Device writes depend on staged manufacturer/site/device-type/role resolution and model-level validation sequencing.
Interface writes include LAG/member behavior, cable relationships, and row-level merge semantics that require adapter-specific logic.

bulk_orm_expansion.message: No additional models should be promoted to bulk ORM until adapter blockers are cleared by parity evidence.
bulk_orm_expansion.next_action: Choose the first recommended promotion lane, write adapter-vs-bulk parity tests for that model family, and enable it only after validation, object-change, Branching, row-issue, and runtime gates pass.

parity_gates.description: Bulk writes must call the same NetBox validation contract and reject the same invalid rows as the adapter path.
Bulk writes must preserve NetBox object-change visibility expected by operators and Branching review.
Bulk writes must produce equivalent Branching diffs, merge behavior, and rollback/discard behavior.
Bulk writes must preserve per-row skip/failure behavior and issue reporting.
Bulk writes must show equal or better runtime on synthetic and large runtime evidence before default enablement.

candidates.blocker_reason: Device writes depend on staged manufacturer/site/device-type/role resolution and model-level validation sequencing.
candidates.evidence_command_hint: Run the candidate-specific adapter-vs-bulk parity tests, then run architecture audit, harness check, and the large-run regression gate before enabling the model.
